name: San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop
name_original: Santuario San Miguel de Aralar – circular desde Irutzun por Ata
description: This difficult 22.1-mile mountain biking loop in Sierra de Aralar offers challenging climbs, rewarding panoramic views, and historical landm
region_intro: Mountain biking around Irurtzun offers a diverse landscape characterized by abrupt and mountainous terrain, situated within the natural corridor of La Barranca, linking the Sierras de Aralar and Andía. The region features the picturesque Larraun River valley, carved through limestone, and dense woodlands of oaks and beeches. This varied topography provides a range of mountain bike trails, from converted railway lines to challenging mountain ascents. Best mountain bike trails around Irurtzun The most popular mountain bike trail is Eltxumendi and San Gregorio Loop via Plazaola Greenway, a 14.2 miles (22.9 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 29 minutes to complete, incorporating sections of the historic Plazaola Greenway. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop, a difficult 22.1 miles (35.6 km) path. This route explores the mountainous terrain of the Aralar range, offering significant elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the Gulina Viaduct – Plazaola Greenway loop from Irurtzun, a 28.1 miles (45.4 km) trail leading through the scenic Plazaola Greenway and past the Gulina Viaduct, often completed in about 4 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Irurtzun is defined by its abrupt and mountainous landscapes, the historic Plazaola Greenway, and the dramatic Dos Hermanas gorge. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate routes along river valleys to difficult climbs in the surrounding sierras. The routes in Irurtzun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Irurtzun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Irurtzun?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Irurtzun, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Irurtzun?
A: Yes, Irurtzun offers trails suitable for less experienced riders and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990036353/eltxumendi-and-san-gregorio-loop-via-plazaola-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eltxumendi and San Gregorio Loop via Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that incorporates sections of the historic Plazaola Greenway, which is generally flatter and more accessible. The Plazaola Greenway itself is a former railway line, making it a good choice for those seeking a less challenging ride, though some sections are unpaved.

Q: What are the most challenging mountain bike routes in Irurtzun?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990042306/san-miguel-de-aralar-and-artxueta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and cover 22.1 miles (35.6 km). Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gulina Viaduct – Plazaola Greenway loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt;, which is 28.1 miles (45.4 km) long and features substantial climbs.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in Irurtzun pass by notable natural features. The dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/15010446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dos Hermanas gorge&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the Larraun River, is a spectacular sight where vultures can often be observed. You can also find trails that lead to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3149161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eltxumendi Summit&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi Summit (1,121 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant attraction, winding through an idyllic river valley.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Irurtzun area?
A: Yes, several mountain bike routes around Irurtzun are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990036353/eltxumendi-and-san-gregorio-loop-via-plazaola-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eltxumendi and San Gregorio Loop via Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway – Plazaola Greenway loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Irurtzun?
A: Irurtzun's diverse vegetation and mountainous terrain make it enjoyable across seasons. However, for optimal conditions, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. While the region can be ridden year-round, be aware that some sections of the Plazaola Greenway are unpaved, which might be less comfortable in very wet conditions.

Q: Is public transport available to Irurtzun for cyclists?
A: While specific details on public transport for bikes to Irurtzun are not readily available, the Navarre region is generally supportive of cyclists. For detailed information on public transport options that accommodate bicycles, it's advisable to check local transport authority websites or contact the Irurtzun town hall directly.

Q: Are there any specific considerations for riding the Plazaola Greenway?
A: The Plazaola Greenway is a fantastic route, but it's important to note that large sections are unpaved, making it best suited for mountain bikes rather than road bikes. Additionally, the route features unlit tunnels, so carrying a bike light is essential for safe passage.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Irurtzun?
A: The mountain bike trails in Irurtzun are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the picturesque Larraun River valley to the challenging ascents in the Sierras de Aralar and Andía, as well as the unique experience of riding along the historic Plazaola Greenway.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ruins accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3552575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixita Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt; are located on a fortified promontory and can be an interesting destination for those looking to combine mountain biking with a touch of history. This site also offers opportunities to observe vultures.

Q: Where can I find services for cyclists, such as bike repair stations, in the Irurtzun area?
A: The Navarre region, where Irurtzun is located, provides services for cyclists, including public self-repair stations equipped with tools and pumps. Some campsites in the wider area may also offer wash areas and workshops. For specific locations in Irurtzun, local tourist information centers would be the best resource.
tips: Picturesque plain at more than 1000 meters above sea level with a path that runs along it and allows you to appreciate the beautiful landscape that surrounds it.

Located between the Aralar mountains, it is a renowned spiritual center and one of the oldest in Navarre. Its 12th century Romanesque architecture is an architectural jewel worth visiting and admiring.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is exceptionally challenging, characterized by significant ascents and descents. You'll encounter narrow, rugged roads, and potentially rougher tracks if venturing off paved sections. The climbs, especially towards Artxueta, feature steep gradients, demanding excellent physical condition and technical skill. Descents are equally challenging, requiring advanced bike handling.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: This loop offers spectacular and expansive views. You'll pass by the ancient San Miguel de Aralar Sanctuary, a 9th-century Romanesque jewel with unbeatable views of the Urbasa and Andía mountain ranges. The Valle de Ata is another scenic point. From the Artxueta Summit (1,340 m), you'll be rewarded with very wide panoramic views of Navarra, offering a pure mountain environment. You'll also traverse diverse landscapes, from dense beech forests to open, high-altitude meadows.

faqs.question: Is the San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is considered very high difficulty and is not suitable for beginners. The relentless and steep gradients, with stretches up to 16-17%, indicate an extreme physical challenge. It demands exceptional strength, endurance, and advanced technical climbing and bike handling skills, especially on unpaved surfaces.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact mountain biking loop aren't detailed, common starting points for routes in the area include the vicinity of the San Miguel de Aralar Sanctuary, which typically has parking facilities, or the town of Irurtzun, from which the Komoot tour starts.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this loop is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October). Due to the high altitude and 'almost Pyrenean atmosphere,' winters can bring snow and ice, making the trail impassable or extremely hazardous. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Sierra de Aralar Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sierra de Aralar Natural Park for activities like mountain biking. However, it's always advisable to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierra de Aralar Natural Park generally allows dogs on its trails. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and livestock, and to ensure the safety and enjoyment of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You might find yourself on parts of the Aralarko bira, Sakanako Donejakue bidea, Ruta ciclable Agurain-Irurtzun Ziklobidea, or the Artzaintza naturbidea, among others. These intersections can offer opportunities for extending or modifying your ride if you're familiar with the area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and mountain environment, you should bring plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate layers of clothing for changing weather conditions, a helmet, and a first-aid kit. A GPS device or a reliable navigation app like Komoot is highly recommended due to potentially varied off-road terrain and forested sections where it's easy to get disoriented.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a pristine mountain environment, you can find amenities in nearby towns. The San Miguel de Aralar Sanctuary often has a small cafe or restaurant. Larger towns like Irurtzun or Lekunberri, which are common access points to the Sierra de Aralar, offer a wider range of cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options.

faqs.question: What makes the climb to Artxueta so challenging?
faqs.answer: The climb to Artxueta is legendary for its steepness and sustained effort. Even on paved sections, it features 'hard ramps' and 'rough asphalt' with constant gradients of 12-17%, some even nearing 15%. For mountain biking, this translates to an exceptionally demanding ascent that tests both physical and mental fortitude, especially if you encounter unpaved tracks.
